mapping.a_element: contact pressure (the stochastic variable whose probability density is solved for)
mapping.b_element: log price (the dynamical variable whose probability density is solved for)

mapping.a_element: magnification / decreasing length scale, the time-like parameter along which the density evolves
mapping.b_element: real time t, the parameter along which the log-price density evolves

mapping.a_element: probability density of contact pressure/interfacial gap P(p, magnification)
the diffusion partial differential equation governing the contact-pressure density (Persson's theory)
mapping.b_element: the Fokker-Planck equation (the no-jump limit of the Kolmogorov-Feller equation)

mapping.a_element: diffusion coefficient fixed by the surface roughness power spectrum

brief_justification: Both reduce their problem to solving a parabolic diffusion / Fokker-Planck PDE for the probability density of a stochastic variable evolving in a time-like parameter -- magnification in Persson's contact theory, real time in the option model -- so the continuous-diffusion machinery maps role-for-role (matching the heat-diffusion analogy calibration case); B additionally carries a jump channel (the Kolmogorov-Feller integral term) plus an entropic-inference/no-arbitrage wrapper that A's purely elastic normal contact lacks, but the shared governing equation is the Fokker-Planck/diffusion PDE.
